name: DealCloud
one_liner: DealCloud is an end-to-end lead and relationship management platform designed for financial and professional services firms, helping them track deals, manage relationships, and streamline workflows throughout the deal lifecycle.
auth_notes: DealCloud uses the OAuth2 Client Credentials flow to generate a bearer token. This token is obtained by sending a POST request to the token endpoint with a client_id and an apiKey (which functions as the client secret). The bearer token is then used in the HTTP Authorization header for subsequent API requests.
access_notes: API access requires an existing DealCloud subscription and administrative approval within the organization's DealCloud instance. A user must be assigned to a user group with appropriate permissions, which an administrator enables. The API key is then obtained from the user's profile. DealCloud is an enterprise platform with custom pricing, typically ranging from $15,000 to $40,000+ per user per year, plus significant implementation costs. There is no indication of free developer accounts or trial access for the API.
api_notes: DealCloud offers a comprehensive set of REST APIs covering various functional areas, including Authentication, User Management, Schema, Data (create, retrieve, update, delete records across objects like deals, contacts, companies), Backups, Publications (real-time events), and Relationship Intelligence. The documentation provides detailed guidance, examples, and best practices. A Python SDK is also available.
mcp_notes: While there's a Workato integration mentioning 'Deal Cloud and MCP (Model Context Protocol) integration,' Workato is an integration platform, not an official or community-endorsed MCP server from DealCloud. No official or well-known community-built MCP server for DealCloud was found in the search results.
blocker: Composio cannot self-serve free or trial API access for development and testing; access requires an existing paid enterprise plan and administrative approval from a DealCloud customer or direct partnership with Intapp.

evidence.claim: DealCloud is an end-to-end lead and relationship management platform for financial and professional services firms.
The DealCloud API uses OAuth2 Client Credentials flow to generate a bearer token, which is then used in the HTTP Authentication header for API requests.
API access requires a user to be assigned to a user group with appropriate permissions, which an administrator enables, and the API key is obtained from the user's profile.
DealCloud is an enterprise platform with custom pricing, typically ranging from $15,000 to $40,000+ per user per year, plus significant implementation costs.
The DealCloud API documentation provides a comprehensive overview of its REST APIs, covering functional areas like User Management, Schema, Data, Backups, Publications, and Relationship Intelligence.
